body.title: Audit telemetry — palace tree + per-run record schema
body.result: Created lab/claudecode/audit_telemetry.py: AuditRunRecord dataclass, emit_run_record() → theigors/audits/<level>/runs/<ts>, emit_watch_next() with TTL, read_runs() + read_watch_next(). Seeded theigors/audits and theigors/audits/model_routing palace nodes. 10 tests pass.
body.description:
Define the structured per-run record every audit emits, and the palace tree it lives in. Enables audit-audits as the consumer.

Palace branches: theigors/audits/<level>/runs/<YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS>, theigors/audits/<level>/watch_next/<id>. Record schema (YAML in node content): level, ran_at, inputs_examined, checks_fired, checks_passed, checks_amended, checks_discarded, findings[{check, severity, file_or_target, matched_pattern, upstream_layer, overridden}], duration_seconds, tokens_used, model, watch_next_written, watch_next_hit, watch_next_aged, watch_next_expired, notes. TTL 90 days for raw runs; promoted patterns persist.

Provide a tiny helper at lab/claudecode/audit_telemetry.py with emit_run_record(level, payload) and read_runs(level, since) — every audit skill calls these. Watch-for notes are first-class palace nodes with a TTL field.

**Affected files:** new lab/claudecode/audit_telemetry.py, new palace subtrees theigors/audits/*. No code in wild_igor/.
**Design rules:** Postgres only — palace lives in memory_palace table. no-new-memory-schemas: reusing memory_palace, no new tables. oop-first: AuditRunRecord class encapsulates emit/read/age. docs-in-code: top-of-file docstring on audit_telemetry.py.
**Test plan:** unit tests for emit_run_record (writes valid palace node), read_runs (returns within window, respects TTL), watch_next age/hit/expire. Integration: emit then read round-trip on real palace.
